rationale: The citing paper is about corporate political advocacy outcomes (market share, customer sorting, authenticity), while the cited paper examines political identity × self-efficacy effects on financial risk-taking; there is thematic overlap on political identity but different focal mechanisms and domains. The only matched evidence is a single DOI reference entry, with no in-text citation context indicating whether it is foundational, methodological, or merely background. Given this sparse evidence, the pair cannot be safely dropped but is not clearly a strong keep.
